svg.text: STORMWEAVER
PRAYAS BHARADWAJ
STORMWEAVER STUDIOS
RESEARCH & DEVELOPMENT LABORATORY
LABORATORY OVERVIEW
StormWeaver Studios is an independent research and development laboratory
focused on high-performance computing, physically accurate rendering systems,
and deterministic deep learning infrastructure. The studio operates with a
research-first methodology and production-grade engineering standards.
Core areas of investigation include massively parallel multithreading, wavelength-
accurate spectral light transport, principled memory management systems, and
deterministic neural inference engines. All systems are engineered for
reproducibility, correctness, and measurable performance under rigorous conditions.
GITHUB ACTIVITY METRICS
RESEARCH DIVISIONS
01 · HIGH-PERFORMANCE COMPUTING DIVISION
Dedicated to the design and implementation of compute infrastructure that is
correct, performant, and reproducible. Systems are engineered to eliminate data
races, memory unsafety, and non-deterministic execution at every level of the stack.
FOCUS AREAS
· Parallel multithreading with full utilisation of available hardware concurrency
· Memory subsystems with shadow tracking, lifetime enforcement, and sanitisation
· Cache-coherent data structures respecting hardware memory hierarchy
· SIMD kernel development for vectorised throughput-critical workloads
· Lock-free concurrency primitives with formal correctness guarantees
· Deterministic execution semantics across all runtime configurations
ACTIVE PROJECTS
StormSTL
— data structures, allocators, and memory primitives
Corium
— parallel runtime and task scheduler
Stratum
— deterministic execution tracing and instrumentation
Kerbecs
— shadow-memory sanitiser and lifetime analyser
Leibniz
— SIMD-optimised mathematics library
Helios-DLX
— deep learning runtime (HPC + DL)
02 · GRAPHICS & RENDERING RESEARCH DIVISION
Focused on physically accurate simulation of light transport across the visible
spectrum. Research spans RGB and spectral rendering paradigms, with active
investigation into neural-assisted reconstruction and denoising pipelines.
FOCUS AREAS
· Path tracing: unidirectional, bidirectional, and Metropolis-based variants
· RGB rendering pipelines for standard display-referred output workflows
· Spectral rendering with wavelength-accurate material and illuminant models
· Light transport theory and Monte Carlo estimator variance reduction
· Neural rendering and learned reconstruction for production-grade output
ACTIVE PROJECTS
Spectra
— research-oriented physically based rendering engine
Spectral & Neural Transport Modules
— wavelength-domain extensions integrated into Spectra
03 · DEEP LEARNING SYSTEMS DIVISION
Concerned with the engineering of neural inference systems built to the same
correctness and performance standards as the broader compute infrastructure.
Deterministic output and numerical reproducibility are non-negotiable requirements.
FOCUS AREAS
· Native deep learning inference without external framework dependencies
· Inference kernels optimised for latency and throughput under constrained resources
· SIMD-accelerated neural arithmetic for CPU-bound execution environments
· Deterministic execution with bit-exact reproducibility across runs
· Custom operator development for workloads not addressed by existing frameworks
ACTIVE PROJECTS
Helios-DLX
— in-house deep learning inference engine
Neural Transport Models
— learned reconstruction models integrated into Spectra
RESEARCH HIGHLIGHT

g.text: DETERMINISTIC SPECTRAL TRANSPORT VIA LINEAR BASIS DECOMPOSITION
A formal framework for deterministic spectral light transport in which spectral
power distributions are decomposed over a fixed global basis. The spectral shape
φ is represented as a basis-space vector, with scalar throughput T tracked
independently. Spectral evolution reduces to a sequence of linear operator
transforms in basis space, eliminating per-path stochastic wavelength sampling.
· Fully deterministic: identical inputs produce identical outputs across all runs
· SIMD-compatible operator structure with natural cache coherence properties
· Eliminates per-path wavelength variance without sacrificing spectral fidelity
The framework interfaces directly with neural reconstruction pipelines, providing
a structured bridge between high-performance compute, physically accurate spectral
physics, and learned inference. Suitable for deployment in production rendering
contexts where correctness and reproducibility are primary engineering constraints.
Integrated into the Spectra rendering engine as the primary spectral transport backend
Operator formalism enables deterministic GPU execution without architectural compromise

svg.text: CORE RESEARCH FOCUS
Deterministic Compute · Spectral Transport · Neural Acceleration
